assessments.title: Life Balance Wheel
assessments.description: Rate your satisfaction across 10 key life domains to visualize where you're thriving and where you need attention.
assessments.instructions: Rate each area of your life from 1 (very dissatisfied) to 10 (deeply fulfilled). Be honest — this is for your eyes only.

domains.name: Physical Health
domains.description: Energy levels, fitness, nutrition, sleep quality, body care
questions: I have consistent energy throughout the day
I exercise regularly and enjoy it
I nourish my body with healthy food
I get quality sleep most nights
I feel physically strong and capable

domains.name: Mental Health
domains.description: Emotional well-being, stress management, inner peace, resilience
questions: I manage stress effectively
I feel emotionally balanced most days
I can sit with difficult emotions without being overwhelmed
I practice self-compassion regularly
I feel a general sense of inner peace

domains.name: Relationships
domains.description: Quality of connections, intimacy, trust, social fulfillment
questions: I have deep, meaningful relationships in my life
I feel seen and understood by people close to me
I communicate openly and honestly
I maintain healthy boundaries
I invest time in nurturing my important relationships

domains.name: Career & Work
domains.description: Professional fulfillment, growth, purpose, work-life balance
questions: My work aligns with my values and strengths
I feel challenged and engaged in my work
I see a clear path for professional growth
I maintain a healthy work-life balance
My work contributes positively to the world

domains.name: Financial Health
domains.description: Financial security, freedom, planning, relationship with money
questions: I feel financially secure
I have a clear financial plan and goals
I spend money in alignment with my values
I am building toward financial freedom
Money does not cause me significant stress

domains.name: Personal Growth
questions: I am actively learning and growing
I regularly step outside my comfort zone
I read or engage with new ideas frequently
I reflect on my experiences and extract lessons
I feel I am becoming a better version of myself

domains.name: Purpose & Meaning
domains.description: Sense of direction, mission, contribution, spiritual fulfillment
questions: I have a clear sense of purpose in my life
My daily actions align with my deeper values
I feel my life has meaning beyond just surviving
I contribute to something larger than myself
I feel connected to a deeper sense of who I am

domains.name: Creativity & Play
domains.description: Creative expression, fun, hobbies, joyful activities
questions: I regularly engage in creative activities
I allow myself time for play and fun
I express myself authentically through some medium
I feel inspired and curious about the world
I don't take life too seriously all the time

domains.name: Physical Environment
domains.description: Living space, surroundings, nature connection, comfort
questions: My living space feels peaceful and organized
I spend time in nature regularly
My environment supports my goals and well-being
I feel safe and comfortable where I live
My surroundings inspire and energize me

domains.name: Contribution & Impact
domains.description: Giving back, service, positive impact on others and the world
questions: I regularly help or serve others
I feel I make a positive difference in people's lives
I contribute to my community in meaningful ways
I am generous with my time, energy, or resources
The world is slightly better because I am in it

assessments.title: Stress & Resilience Check
assessments.description: Assess your current stress levels and resilience capacity to understand where you need support.
assessments.instructions: For each statement, rate how true it has been for you in the past two weeks (1 = Not at all, 5 = Extremely true).

questions.text: I feel overwhelmed by my responsibilities
I have trouble sleeping due to worry or racing thoughts
I can bounce back quickly from setbacks
I feel tension in my body (shoulders, jaw, stomach)
I have people I can turn to when I need support
I feel like I'm constantly running but getting nowhere
I maintain perspective when things go wrong
I often feel irritable or short-tempered
I have healthy coping strategies for difficult emotions
I struggle to be present — my mind is always elsewhere
I believe challenges help me grow
I use food, alcohol, or screens to numb difficult feelings
I take time for activities that recharge me
I feel anxious about the future
I can find meaning even in difficult experiences
I feel disconnected from the people around me
I practice self-care without guilt
I feel like I'm on autopilot most days
I face problems head-on rather than avoiding them
I have difficulty concentrating on tasks

low.label: Low Stress
low.message: You're managing well. Your stress levels are healthy. Continue with your current practices and stay mindful of any changes.

moderate.label: Moderate Stress
moderate.message: You're experiencing a noticeable level of stress. Consider incorporating daily breathwork, increased physical activity, and dedicated downtime into your routine.

high.label: High Stress
high.message: Your stress levels are elevated. Prioritize stress management — daily breathing exercises, journaling, physical movement, and consider talking to a trusted person or professional.

developing.label: Building Resilience
developing.message: Your resilience muscles are developing. Focus on building supportive relationships, practicing gratitude, and developing a growth mindset through small daily challenges.

moderate.label: Moderate Resilience
moderate.message: You have a solid foundation of resilience. Strengthen it further through consistent practice — meditation, journaling, and intentional reflection on challenges.

strong.label: Strong Resilience
strong.message: Your resilience is a significant strength. You have effective coping strategies and a healthy relationship with adversity. Share these skills with others and continue refining them.

assessments.title: Growth Mindset Assessment
assessments.description: Discover whether you tend toward a fixed or growth mindset — and learn how to cultivate the belief system that underlies all lasting change.
assessments.instructions: Rate how much you agree with each statement (1 = Strongly Disagree, 5 = Strongly Agree).

questions.text: My intelligence and abilities can be developed with effort and practice
When I fail at something, I see it as a learning opportunity
I tend to avoid challenges that might make me look incompetent
I believe talent is more important than effort
Criticism from others helps me improve
I feel threatened by the success of others
I enjoy being challenged and stretched beyond my comfort zone
When something is hard, I assume it's not meant for me
I believe effort is the path to mastery
I tend to give up when I encounter obstacles
I'm inspired by the success of others
I believe people can fundamentally change who they are
I avoid trying new things because I might not be good at them
The harder I work at something, the better I get
My core qualities are fixed traits that I can't change much

growth_dominant.label: Growth Mindset
growth_dominant.message: You predominantly operate from a growth mindset. You see challenges as opportunities, believe in the power of effort, and view failure as feedback. This mindset is the foundation of continuous improvement. Nurture it.

mixed.label: Mixed Mindset
mixed.message: Like most people, you show elements of both mindsets. In some areas you embrace growth; in others, fixed beliefs hold you back. Identify the specific domains where fixed thinking limits you and start challenging those beliefs.

fixed_dominant.label: Fixed Mindset Tendency
fixed_dominant.message: You may be held back by beliefs that your abilities are set in stone. The good news? Mindset itself is changeable. Start by reframing challenges as growth opportunities and celebrating effort over outcomes.
